item.title: Cambodia tracking down thousands of priceless looted antiquities | 60 Minutes
item.description: Looters stole thousands of priceless artifacts from religious sites across Cambodia. An American lawyer is working with the country to bring them home.

item.title: High win rate of bets on military operations a likely sign of insider trading
item.description: Prediction market users are profiting from bets on war. Analysts say suspiciously timed bets, and the high win rates of wagers on military outcomes , are likely signs of insider trading.

item.title: Searching for Cambodia's stolen crown jewels
item.description: Crowns and precious jewels believed to have been worn by ancient Khmer royalty were tracked down to an unlikely place: a pub parking lot outside London.

item.title: How stolen Cambodian artifacts ended up in American museums
item.description: Looters stole thousands of priceless artifacts from religious sites across Cambodia. An American lawyer is working with the country to bring them home

item.title: Suspicious trades make millions with bets tied to U.S. military operations
item.description: Prediction market users are profiting from bets on war. Analysts say suspiciously timed bets, and the high win rates of wagers on military outcomes , are likely signs of insider trading.

item.title: Delaware Sen. Chris Coons injured in Sussex County car crash
item.description: Delaware Sen. Chris Coons was injured in a crash that involved several vehicles in Sussex County Sunday afternoon, he announced on social media.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Pakistan says its security forces killed 29 militants along Afghan border
item.description: Pakistani security forces Sunday carried out an intelligence-based ground operation along the Pakistan-Afghanistan​ border, followed by "calibrated strikes."

item.title: Videos show U.S. rescue team pull mother, baby from rubble in Venezuela
item.description: Hundreds of U.S. search and rescue workers are on the ground in Venezuela after deadly quakes struck the South American nation.

item.title: Iran launches drone, missile attacks targeting Bahrain, Kuwait
item.description: The strikes in Kuwait and Bahrain came after the US and Iran traded attacks over the weekend.

item.title: 3 firefighters killed, 2 injured fighting wildfires near Colorado-Utah border
item.description: The U.S. Wildland Fire Service said the firefighters had been part of an interagency response to the Knowles and Gore fires near the Colorado-Utah border.

item.title: Full transcript of "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," June 28, 2026
item.description: On this "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" broadcast, Sens. Bill Cassidy and Tim Kaine join Margaret Brennan.

item.title: Alan Jackson gives emotional farewell concert
item.description: Five years ago, Alan Jackson shared that he has a degenerative nerve condition that affects his balance called Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease, which he was first diagnosed with a decade prior.

item.title: Dangerous heat wave impacting large swathes of the U.S. this week
item.description: A heat wave will blast a large swath of the U.S. this week. The National Weather Service says temperatures will feel hotter because of the high humidity that's arriving with it.

item.title: Kaine says guardrails on Pentagon firings could see bipartisan support
item.description: Sen. Tim Kaine said guardrails on Pentagon firings could see bipartisan support in Congress, following a string of high-level officers exiting the military during the second Trump administration.

item.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy accuses RFK Jr. of building public health "upon a foundation of lies"
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy, who delivered the key vote to advance Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s nomination, told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that "if you build public health upon a foundation of lies, then you're going to have the absence of adequate public health."

item.title: Transcript: Sen. Tim Kaine on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," June 28, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of an interview with Sen. Tim Kaine, Democrat of Virginia, that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on June 28, 2026.

item.title: Jan Crawford says remaining Supreme Court decisions could be a "mixed bag" for Trump
item.description: Jan Crawford, CBS News' chief legal correspondent, says that she believes the remaining Supreme Court decisions will be a "mixed bag" for President Trump.

item.title: Face the Nation: Kaine, Cassidy, Crawford
item.description: Missed the second half of the show? Sens. Tim Kaine and Bill Cassidy and CBS News chief legal correspondent Jan Crawford join.

item.title: Sen. Tim Kaine says guardrails on Pentagon firings could see bipartisan support in Congress
item.description: Democratic Sen. Tim Kaine of Virginia told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that he believes there could be bipartisan support for guardrails on Pentagon firings in the aftermath of the departure of Gen. Chris Donahue, the commander of U.S. Army Europe and Africa.

item.title: 5 nurses among 11 killed in French skydiving plane crash
item.description: Mayor Mathieu Klein said the victims "died in full view of their loved ones, who were preparing to film the tandem skydives."

item.title: Larry David on "Life, Larry, and the Pursuit of Unhappiness"
item.description: The comic icon behind "Curb Your Enthusiasm" brings his own perspective to America's storied past in a new HBO sketch comedy series – finally making use of his history major from college.

item.title: 6/28: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," Sen. Bill Cassidy discusses the explosive meeting between President Trump and Senate Republicans as well as why he changed his vote on the Iran war powers resolution. Plus, Sen. Tim Kaine and CBS News chief legal correspondent Jan Crawford join.

item.title: Governors Island: An oasis in New York Harbor
item.description: One of New York City's most unique public spaces is a 172-acre island off the tip of Manhattan. Jane Pauley offers a tour of Governors Island, celebrated for its parks, cultural events, and sweeping harbor views.

item.title: Poll: America at 250 and some of what Americans think is best about U.S.
item.description: Americans weighed in on what's best about the U.S., its greatest invention, most representative food and more in latest CBS News poll.

item.title: NASA prepares to launch rescue to save telescope from falling to Earth
item.description: The $30 million salvage operation gets underway as soon as this week with the planned launch of a robotic lifesaver.

item.title: Historian retraces Underground Railroad to mark America's 250-year journey
item.description: Anthony Cohen has spent his life bringing attention to the Underground Railroad, secret routes once used by enslaved people seeking freedom.

item.title: Judge in Charlie Kirk murder case finds prosecutor in contempt
item.description: The defense team for Tyler Robinson asked that the death penalty be taken off the table following public comments by prosecutors.

item.title: Amy Sherald on "Ecclesia," her most ambitious work yet
item.description: American Realist portraitist Amy Sherald showed Anderson Cooper her evocative "Ecclesia," a triptych that is much larger in scale than earlier paintings.

item.title: Why the COVID vaccine isn't part of an injury compensation court
item.description: A federal court compensates people who say they were injured by vaccines. Here is why the COVID vaccines are not currently part of it.
